name: Apricot Crumbcake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Butter a 10-inch round, 2-inch deep cake pan and line the bottom with a disk of parchment or wax paper.
3. In a large mixing bowl, beat the butter and sugar until soft and light.
4. Add the egg and continue beating until light.
5. Add the yolks one at a time, beating well between each addition.
6. Beat in the vanilla extract.
7. Sift together the flour and baking powder and stir into the batter.
8. Spread the batter evenly in the prepared pan.
9. Rinse and halve the apricots. Remove the pits with the bowl of a small spoon if they resist.
10. Arrange the apricot halves cut-side up on the batter in concentric rows, overlapping slightly, leaving a margin of about 1-inch around the edge.
11. For the crumb topping, mix together 1 cup All-purpose flour, 1/2 cup Sugar, and 1/4 tsp Cinnamon in a bowl.
12. Melt 1/2 cup Unsalted butter and stir it in evenly with the dry ingredients.
13. Rub the mixture to coarse crumbs by hand.
14. Scatter the crumb topping over the apricots and the edge of the batter as evenly as possible.
15. Bake the cake for 1 hour until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean.
16. Cool the cake in the pan for about 15 minutes, then invert a plate on it.
17. Invert the cake onto the plate and lift off the pan.
18. Peel off the parchment or wax paper and invert a rack on the cake.
19. Invert the cake onto the rack, remove the plate, and cool.
Use this recipe with peaches, sour cherries, or pineapple for equally good results.
